sections.paragraph:
ξόανον, ου, τό (Trag. et al.; ins, pap) a wooden cult image (so Eur.; X., An. 5, 3, 12; ins, pap; Aq. Ezk 6:4; Manetho in Jos., C. Ap. 1, 244; 249; Philo, Mos. 1, 298 al.; Ath. 4, 1; SibOr 3, 723) ApcPt 18:33.—DELG s.v. ξέω.

Trag.

Trag. = writer (s) of tragedy

trag. = tragedy, writer (s) of tragedy

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

Eur Eur , V b.c.—List 5

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Aq Aq , II a.d.—List 5

Manetho Manetho , hist., III b.c.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

SibOr SibOr = Oracula Sibyllina, II–III a.d.—List 5

ApcPt ApcPt = Apocalypse of Peter—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 685
